---
title: "Final Project — Static Plots"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import geopandas as gpd
import altair as alt
```


```{python}
# =========================================================
# 0) Paths & shared utilities  (RELATIVE PATHS ONLY)
# =========================================================

from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import geopandas as gpd
import altair as alt


def ensure_exists(path: Path, what: str) -> None:
    if not path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"[Missing {what}] Cannot find: {path}")


def ensure_cols(df: pd.DataFrame, required: list[str], where: str) -> None:
    missing = [c for c in required if c not in df.columns]
    if missing:
        raise ValueError(f"[{where}] Missing required columns: {missing}")


def clean_key(s: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    return s.astype(str).str.strip()


def to_numeric_inplace(df: pd.DataFrame, cols: list[str]) -> None:
    for c in cols:
        if c in df.columns:
            df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")


def safe_log_income(df: pd.DataFrame, income_col: str = "median_income_proxy") -> pd.DataFrame:
    df = df.copy()
    if income_col not in df.columns:
        raise ValueError(f"Income column '{income_col}' not found.")
    df[income_col] = pd.to_numeric(df[income_col], errors="coerce")
    df = df.dropna(subset=[income_col])
    df = df[df[income_col] > 0]
    df["log_income"] = np.log(df[income_col])
    return df


def save_fig(fig: plt.Figure, outdir: Path, filename: str, dpi: int = 200) -> Path:
    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out = outdir / filename
    fig.savefig(out, dpi=dpi, bbox_inches="tight")
    return out


REPO = Path("..")

if not (REPO / "data").is_dir() or not (REPO / "code").is_dir():
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        "Expected structure: ../data and ../code (run this QMD from code/)."
    )

RAW_NTA_SHP = REPO / "data" / "raw-data" / "nta_2020" / "nta_2020.shp"
DERIVED = REPO / "data" / "derived-data"
OUTDIR = REPO / "figures"

INCOME_CSV = DERIVED / "nta_income_households_2024.csv"
SUMMARY_CSV = DERIVED / "nta_inspection_summary.csv"
INSP_PARQUET = DERIVED / "inspections_with_nta_income.parquet"

print("REPO         =", REPO)
print("RAW_NTA_SHP  =", RAW_NTA_SHP)
print("INCOME_CSV   =", INCOME_CSV)
print("SUMMARY_CSV  =", SUMMARY_CSV)
print("INSP_PARQUET =", INSP_PARQUET)
print("OUTDIR       =", OUTDIR)
```


```{python}
# =========================================================
# 1) Load NTA geometry
# =========================================================

ensure_exists(RAW_NTA_SHP, "NTA shapefile")
nta_gdf = gpd.read_file(RAW_NTA_SHP)

NTA_CODE_COL = "nta2020"
ensure_cols(nta_gdf, [NTA_CODE_COL], "NTA shapefile")

nta_gdf = nta_gdf.copy()
nta_gdf["nta"] = clean_key(nta_gdf[NTA_CODE_COL])

nta_gdf = nta_gdf.to_crs(epsg=4326)

nta_gdf.head(2)
```

```{python}
# =========================================================
# 2) One generic Altair choropleth function
# =========================================================

def plot_nta_choropleth_altair(
    gdf: gpd.GeoDataFrame,
    value_col: str,
    title: str,
    legend_title: str,
    scheme: str = "viridis",
    tooltip_format: str = ",.2f",
    output_html: str | None = None,
    width: int = 700,
    height: int = 700,
) -> alt.Chart:
    if value_col not in gdf.columns:
        raise ValueError(f"'{value_col}' not in GeoDataFrame columns.")

    geojson = gdf.__geo_interface__

    chart = (
        alt.Chart(alt.Data(values=geojson["features"]))
        .mark_geoshape(stroke="white", strokeWidth=0.3)
        .encode(
            color=alt.Color(
                f"properties.{value_col}:Q",
                title=legend_title,
                scale=alt.Scale(scheme=scheme),
            ),
            tooltip=[
                alt.Tooltip("properties.nta:N", title="NTA"),
                alt.Tooltip(
                    f"properties.{value_col}:Q",
                    title=legend_title,
                    format=tooltip_format
                ),
            ],
        )
        .properties(title=title, width=width, height=height)
        .project(type="mercator")
    )

    if output_html is not None:
        outpath = OUTDIR / output_html
        outpath.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        chart.save(str(outpath))
        print("[Saved]", outpath)

    return chart
```



```{python}
# =========================================================
# Map 1: NYC NTA Median Household Income
# =========================================================

ensure_exists(INCOME_CSV, "income CSV")
income_df = pd.read_csv(INCOME_CSV)

ensure_cols(income_df, ["nta", "median_income_proxy"], "income CSV")
income_df = income_df.copy()
income_df["nta"] = clean_key(income_df["nta"])
to_numeric_inplace(income_df, ["median_income_proxy"])

gdf_income = nta_gdf.merge(income_df[["nta", "median_income_proxy"]], on="nta", how="left")
print("Share missing income after merge:", f"{gdf_income['median_income_proxy'].isna().mean():.2%}")

chart1 = plot_nta_choropleth_altair(
    gdf_income,
    value_col="median_income_proxy",
    title="NYC NTA Median Household Income (Proxy)",
    legend_title="Median household income",
    scheme="viridis",
    tooltip_format=",.0f",
    output_html="nta_income_map.html",
)
chart1
```


```{python}
# =========================================================
# Map 2: NYC NTA Food Safety Risk (Average Inspection Score)
# =========================================================

ensure_exists(SUMMARY_CSV, "inspection summary CSV")
summ = pd.read_csv(SUMMARY_CSV)

ensure_cols(summ, ["nta", "avg_score"], "summary CSV")
summ = summ.copy()
summ["nta"] = clean_key(summ["nta"])
to_numeric_inplace(summ, ["avg_score"])

gdf_score = nta_gdf.merge(summ[["nta", "avg_score"]], on="nta", how="left")
print("Share missing avg_score after merge:", f"{gdf_score['avg_score'].isna().mean():.2%}")

chart2 = plot_nta_choropleth_altair(
    gdf_score,
    value_col="avg_score",
    title="NYC NTA Food Risk (Average Inspection Score)",
    legend_title="Avg score (higher = worse)",
    scheme="reds",
    tooltip_format=",.2f",
    output_html="nta_avg_score_map.html",
)
chart2
```


```{python}
# =========================================================
# Map 3: NYC Restaurant Inspection Intensity by NTA
# =========================================================

ensure_cols(summ, ["nta", "n_inspections", "n_unique_restaurants"], "summary CSV (intensity)")
to_numeric_inplace(summ, ["n_inspections", "n_unique_restaurants"])

summ["inspection_intensity"] = summ["n_inspections"] / summ["n_unique_restaurants"]
summ.loc[summ["n_unique_restaurants"] == 0, "inspection_intensity"] = np.nan
summ["inspection_intensity"] = summ["inspection_intensity"].replace([np.inf, -np.inf], np.nan)

gdf_intensity = nta_gdf.merge(
    summ[["nta", "inspection_intensity", "n_inspections", "n_unique_restaurants"]],
    on="nta",
    how="left",
)
print("Share missing intensity after merge:", f"{gdf_intensity['inspection_intensity'].isna().mean():.2%}")

chart3 = plot_nta_choropleth_altair(
    gdf_intensity,
    value_col="inspection_intensity",
    title="NYC Restaurant Inspection Intensity (Inspections per Restaurant)",
    legend_title="Inspections / restaurant",
    scheme="viridis",
    tooltip_format=",.2f",
    output_html="nta_inspection_intensity_map.html",
)
chart3
```




```{python}
# =========================================================
# 3) Static plots (scatter + stacked + trend) -- Altair HTML ONLY
# =========================================================

def save_chart_html(chart: alt.Chart, outdir: Path, filename: str) -> Path:
    """
    Save Altair chart as HTML (always). Return saved path.
    """
    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    outpath = outdir / filename
    chart.save(str(outpath))
    print("[Saved]", outpath)
    return outpath


def scatter_with_fit_altair(
    d: pd.DataFrame,
    x: str,
    y: str,
    xlabel: str,
    ylabel: str,
    title: str,
    out_html: str,
    x_domain: tuple[float, float] = (10, 13),
    x_ticks: list[float] = [10, 11, 12, 13],
    width: int = 600,
    height: int = 420,
) -> alt.Chart:
    """
    Scatter + OLS regression line (red) using Altair.
    Force x-axis domain and ticks (for log_income aesthetics).
    Save as HTML and return the chart (for Quarto display).
    """
    if x not in d.columns or y not in d.columns:
        raise ValueError(f"scatter_with_fit_altair needs columns: {x}, {y}")

    dd = d[[x, y]].copy()
    dd[x] = pd.to_numeric(dd[x], errors="coerce")
    dd[y] = pd.to_numeric(dd[y], errors="coerce")
    dd = dd[np.isfinite(dd[x]) & np.isfinite(dd[y])].copy()
    if len(dd) < 2:
        raise ValueError(f"Not enough valid rows to plot: {title}")

    x_enc = alt.X(
        f"{x}:Q",
        title=xlabel,
        scale=alt.Scale(domain=list(x_domain)),
        axis=alt.Axis(values=x_ticks, format=".0f"),
    )

    base = alt.Chart(dd).properties(title=title, width=width, height=height)

    points = base.mark_circle(opacity=0.6).encode(
        x=x_enc,
        y=alt.Y(f"{y}:Q", title=ylabel),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip(f"{x}:Q", title=xlabel, format=",.2f"),
            alt.Tooltip(f"{y}:Q", title=ylabel, format=",.2f"),
        ],
    )

    # regression line in red
    line = base.transform_regression(x, y).mark_line(color="red", strokeWidth=2.5).encode(
        x=x_enc,
        y=alt.Y(f"{y}:Q"),
    )

    chart = (points + line).interactive()
    save_chart_html(chart, OUTDIR, out_html)
    return chart


# ---- Load summary again ----
df = pd.read_csv(SUMMARY_CSV).copy()
ensure_cols(
    df,
    ["nta", "median_income_proxy", "n_inspections", "n_unique_restaurants", "avg_score"],
    "summary CSV (static plots)",
)
df["nta"] = clean_key(df["nta"])
to_numeric_inplace(df, ["median_income_proxy", "n_inspections", "n_unique_restaurants", "avg_score"])

df["inspection_intensity"] = df["n_inspections"] / df["n_unique_restaurants"]
df.loc[df["n_unique_restaurants"] == 0, "inspection_intensity"] = np.nan
df["inspection_intensity"] = df["inspection_intensity"].replace([np.inf, -np.inf], np.nan)

df = safe_log_income(df, "median_income_proxy")

# ---- Scatter 1: log income vs intensity ----
chart_scatter_income_intensity = scatter_with_fit_altair(
    df,
    x="log_income",
    y="inspection_intensity",
    xlabel="Log Median Household Income (proxy)",
    ylabel="Inspection Intensity (Inspections per Restaurant)",
    title="Income vs Inspection Intensity (NTA)",
    out_html="scatter_income_vs_intensity.html",
    x_domain=(10, 13),
    x_ticks=[10, 11, 12, 13],
)

# ---- Scatter 2: log income vs avg score ----
chart_scatter_income_score = scatter_with_fit_altair(
    df,
    x="log_income",
    y="avg_score",
    xlabel="Log Median Household Income (proxy)",
    ylabel="Average Inspection Score (higher = worse)",
    title="Income vs Average Score (NTA)",
    out_html="scatter_income_vs_score.html",
    x_domain=(10, 13),
    x_ticks=[10, 11, 12, 13],
)

# ---- Scatter 3: intensity vs avg score (no forced x range here) ----
def scatter_with_fit_altair_simple(
    d: pd.DataFrame,
    x: str,
    y: str,
    xlabel: str,
    ylabel: str,
    title: str,
    out_html: str,
    width: int = 600,
    height: int = 420,
) -> alt.Chart:
    dd = d[[x, y]].copy()
    dd[x] = pd.to_numeric(dd[x], errors="coerce")
    dd[y] = pd.to_numeric(dd[y], errors="coerce")
    dd = dd[np.isfinite(dd[x]) & np.isfinite(dd[y])].copy()
    if len(dd) < 2:
        raise ValueError(f"Not enough valid rows to plot: {title}")

    base = alt.Chart(dd).properties(title=title, width=width, height=height)

    points = base.mark_circle(opacity=0.6).encode(
        x=alt.X(f"{x}:Q", title=xlabel),
        y=alt.Y(f"{y}:Q", title=ylabel),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip(f"{x}:Q", title=xlabel, format=",.2f"),
            alt.Tooltip(f"{y}:Q", title=ylabel, format=",.2f"),
        ],
    )

    line = base.transform_regression(x, y).mark_line(color="red", strokeWidth=2.5).encode(
        x=alt.X(f"{x}:Q"),
        y=alt.Y(f"{y}:Q"),
    )

    chart = (points + line).interactive()
    save_chart_html(chart, OUTDIR, out_html)
    return chart


chart_scatter_intensity_score = scatter_with_fit_altair_simple(
    df,
    x="inspection_intensity",
    y="avg_score",
    xlabel="Inspection Intensity (Inspections per Restaurant)",
    ylabel="Average Inspection Score (higher = worse)",
    title="Inspection Intensity vs Average Score (NTA)",
    out_html="scatter_intensity_vs_score.html",
)

# Show in Quarto HTML
chart_scatter_income_intensity

chart_scatter_income_score

chart_scatter_intensity_score


# =========================================================
# 4) Stacked bar: income quintile vs inspection stage -- Altair HTML ONLY
# =========================================================

def extract_stage(inspection_type: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    split = inspection_type.astype(str).str.split("/", n=1, expand=True)
    if split.shape[1] == 2:
        stage = split[1].str.strip()
        stage = stage.where(stage.notna() & (stage != ""), "Unknown")
        return stage
    return pd.Series(["Unknown"] * len(inspection_type), index=inspection_type.index)


ensure_exists(INSP_PARQUET, "inspections parquet")
insp = pd.read_parquet(INSP_PARQUET).copy()

ensure_cols(insp, ["median_income_proxy", "INSPECTION TYPE"], "inspections parquet (stacked stage)")

insp["median_income_proxy"] = pd.to_numeric(insp["median_income_proxy"], errors="coerce")
insp = insp.dropna(subset=["median_income_proxy", "INSPECTION TYPE"])
insp = insp[insp["median_income_proxy"] > 0]

insp["income_quintile"] = pd.qcut(
    insp["median_income_proxy"],
    5,
    labels=["Q1 (Lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 (Highest)"],
)

insp["inspection_stage"] = extract_stage(insp["INSPECTION TYPE"])

top_stages = insp["inspection_stage"].value_counts().index[:5]
insp["inspection_stage"] = insp["inspection_stage"].where(insp["inspection_stage"].isin(top_stages), "Other")

stage_share = (
    insp.groupby(["income_quintile", "inspection_stage"])
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="n")
)
stage_share["share"] = stage_share.groupby("income_quintile")["n"].transform(lambda s: s / s.sum())

chart_stage = (
    alt.Chart(stage_share)
    .mark_bar()
    .encode(
        x=alt.X(
            "income_quintile:N",
            title="Income Quintile",
            sort=["Q1 (Lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 (Highest)"],
        ),
        y=alt.Y("share:Q", title="Share of Inspections", axis=alt.Axis(format="%")),
        color=alt.Color("inspection_stage:N", title="Inspection Stage"),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("income_quintile:N", title="Income Quintile"),
            alt.Tooltip("inspection_stage:N", title="Stage"),
            alt.Tooltip("n:Q", title="Count", format=",.0f"),
            alt.Tooltip("share:Q", title="Share", format=".2%"),
        ],
    )
    .properties(
        title="Income Quintile vs Inspection Stage (Stacked Share)",
        width=700,
        height=420,
    )
    .interactive()
)

save_chart_html(chart_stage, OUTDIR, "stacked_income_quintile_vs_stage.html")
chart_stage


# =========================================================
# 5) Trend: slope of (intensity ~ log_income) by year -- Altair HTML ONLY
# =========================================================

def get_inspection_date(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.Series:
    if "inspection_date" in df.columns:
        return pd.to_datetime(df["inspection_date"], errors="coerce")
    if "INSPECTION DATE" in df.columns:
        return pd.to_datetime(df["INSPECTION DATE"], errors="coerce")
    raise ValueError("Need 'inspection_date' or 'INSPECTION DATE' column.")


dfi = pd.read_parquet(INSP_PARQUET).copy()
dfi["inspection_date"] = get_inspection_date(dfi)

dfi = dfi[dfi["inspection_date"].notna()]
dfi = dfi[dfi["inspection_date"] != pd.Timestamp("1900-01-01")]
dfi["year"] = dfi["inspection_date"].dt.year

ensure_cols(dfi, ["nta", "CAMIS", "median_income_proxy"], "inspections parquet (trend slope)")
dfi["nta"] = clean_key(dfi["nta"])
dfi["median_income_proxy"] = pd.to_numeric(dfi["median_income_proxy"], errors="coerce")
dfi = dfi.dropna(subset=["nta", "CAMIS", "median_income_proxy"])
dfi = dfi[dfi["median_income_proxy"] > 0]
dfi["log_income"] = np.log(dfi["median_income_proxy"])

nta_year = (
    dfi.groupby(["year", "nta"], as_index=False)
    .agg(
        n_inspections=("CAMIS", "size"),
        n_unique_restaurants=("CAMIS", "nunique"),
        log_income=("log_income", "first"),
    )
)
nta_year["inspection_intensity"] = nta_year["n_inspections"] / nta_year["n_unique_restaurants"]
nta_year = nta_year[
    np.isfinite(nta_year["inspection_intensity"]) & np.isfinite(nta_year["log_income"])
].copy()

min_nta = 30
results = []
for year in sorted(nta_year["year"].unique()):
    sub = nta_year[nta_year["year"] == year]
    if len(sub) < min_nta:
        continue
    z = np.polyfit(sub["log_income"], sub["inspection_intensity"], 1)
    results.append({"year": int(year), "slope": float(z[0]), "n_nta": int(len(sub))})

slopes = pd.DataFrame(results)
if slopes.empty:
    raise ValueError("No year has enough NTA observations to estimate slopes.")

csvpath = OUTDIR / "trend_slope_intensity_on_income_by_year.csv"
slopes.to_csv(csvpath, index=False)
print("[Saved]", csvpath)

chart_trend = (
    alt.Chart(slopes)
    .mark_line()
    .encode(
        x=alt.X("year:O", title="Year"),
        y=alt.Y("slope:Q", title="Slope of intensity ~ log_income"),
        tooltip=[
            alt.Tooltip("year:O", title="Year"),
            alt.Tooltip("slope:Q", title="Slope", format=",.4f"),
            alt.Tooltip("n_nta:Q", title="N NTA", format=",.0f"),
        ],
    )
    .properties(
        title="Income–Inspection Intensity Relationship Over Time",
        width=700,
        height=420,
    )
)

zero = alt.Chart(pd.DataFrame({"y": [0]})).mark_rule().encode(y="y:Q")

chart_trend_final = (zero + chart_trend + chart_trend.mark_circle(size=60)).interactive()

save_chart_html(chart_trend_final, OUTDIR, "trend_slope_intensity_on_income_by_year.html")
chart_trend_final

```
